title: Toiyabea eximia
intro: Toiyabea eximia, also known as the Toiyabe buckwheat or Sierra buckwheat, is a rare and perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The bioactive constituents of Toiyabea eximia include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Toiyabea eximia belongs to the family of flowering plants known as Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant endemic to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The exact classification of Toiyabea eximia may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the genus Toiyabea and the order Asterales. More specifically, it is classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae within the family Asteraceae. Further classification details, such as its tribe and genus, are still being researched and updated in botanical databases.
